findings.description: The NC Waste Management logo in the HTML version appears slightly different in rendering but is present and correctly placed.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC logo with 'Waste Management ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' text beneath. HTML shows the same logo in a similar position.

findings.description: The MEMORANDUM heading in the HTML uses a different underline style (horizontal rule) compared to the PDF's simple underline.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'MEMORANDUM' with traditional underline. HTML: 'MEMORANDUM' with a bold horizontal rule beneath it.

findings.description: Digital signature information appears in the HTML version that is not visible in the PDF version's first page.
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner' with DN and date details. PDF page 1 does not show this digital signature metadata.

findings.description: The HTML combines what appears as a page break continuation into a continuous scroll, showing footer and header elements inline.
findings.evidence: PDF has clear page breaks between pages 1 and 2. HTML shows the footer/header information inline between content sections.

findings.description: The report outline numbering (I through IV) uses slightly different indentation in HTML vs PDF but content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF has Roman numerals indented with tab stops. HTML renders them as a simple list with different indentation.

findings.description: Section heading 'I. History/Background' formatting differs slightly - PDF uses bold with Roman numeral separated, HTML uses a larger heading style.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I. History/Background' in bold with standard formatting. HTML: rendered as a larger section heading.

findings.description: Bullet point topic labels in the oral comments summary use underline+bold in PDF but bold only in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Excavation' is underlined and bold. HTML: 'Excavation' appears bold but not underlined.

findings.description: The HTML uses h1 for the MEMORANDUM heading, but then uses h2 for sections I, II, III, and IV. The PDF document uses a clear Roman numeral section structure (I, II, III, IV) which are all at the same level. Additionally, 'MEMORANDUM' is styled as h1 with bold and underline, which is acceptable as a document title, but the nesting of a second <main> element inside the first <main> is a structural HTML issue rather than a heading hierarchy problem per se.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

MEMORANDUM

followed by

I. History/Background

,

II. March 16, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary

, etc. This is acceptable heading hierarchy.

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested <main> element inside the outer <main>, which creates structural duplication. The page 1 content including the memorandum header info, outline, and footer are presented, followed by repeat of the same structural content across pages. However, this reflects the page-by-page structure and the content itself is not duplicated — this is a minor structural HTML issue.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> wrapping everything, then another <main> element inside for page 1 content.

findings.description: The second image has an empty alt text attribute, which is not descriptive. The first image has reasonable alt text.
findings.evidence: HTML: for the second image (appears to be a decorative line/footer image). First image: alt="NC Waste Management Environmental Quality logo" which is appropriate.

findings.description: The digital signature details in the HTML include 'email=elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov' which matches the PDF's digital signature block showing 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov'. The PDF header footer text and all substantive content matches. No fabricated content found.
findings.evidence: All text in HTML traces back to the PDF source. Verified against all sections, bullet points, statistics (207 attendees, 33 speakers, 26 registered, 643 emails, 8 hand-submitted, 287,000 people, 1500-ft boundary), proper nouns, and dates.
